summary: Training run crashed due to loss NaN exception inside custom transformer transformer layers
description: Training anomaly: deep learning run diverged unexpectedly mid-epoch causing a sudden validation spike and standard loss NaN exception behavior. Dataset version: isolated down to recent parquet file processing splits uploaded on the master s3 dataset branch. Hardware fault: telemetry indicates a sudden spike prior to a cuda out of memory / oom failure on node 4. Telemetry trace: detailed evaluation graph captures can be found inside the integrated wandb reporting link.

embeddings"}, "niceToHave_fields": {"hyperparameters": "learning rate, batch size, epochs, optimizer","registry_path": "mlflow, huggingface, weights, artifact"}},"story": {"mandatory_fields": {"inference_target": "latency, throughput, realtime api, batch inference","monitoring_rule": "data drift, concept drift, threshold, skew"}, "niceToHave_fields": {"fallback_strategy": "shadow deployment, canary routing, backup model","optimization_type": "quantization, int8, tensorrt, ONNX"}},"bug": {"mandatory_fields": {"training_anomaly": "gradient explosion, loss NaN, validation spike, overfitting","dataset_version": "huggingface hub, s3 dataset, parquet file"}, "niceToHave_fields": {"hardware_fault": "cuda out of memory, oom, kernel panic, throttling","telemetry_trace": "wandb, tensorboard, prometheus"}},"epic": {"mandatory_fields": {"mlops_objective": "automated retraining, pipeline orchestration, serving infrastructure","governance_framework": "eu ai act, model lineage, summary: Provision redundant multi-region AWS infrastructure clusters using Terraform modules
description: Deployment target: orchestrate high-availability AWS EKS compute nodes across eu-west-1 and us-east-1 zones. State storage: configure a secure S3 storage bucket with active dynamo-db row locking policies. Resource scaling: implement auto-scaling parameters capping maximum node growth. Destruction policy: retain state backups upon deletion.
